sections.paragraph:
ἀποκρύπτω fut. ἀποκρύψω; 1 aor. ἀπέκρυψα. Pass.: 1 fut. ἀποκρυβήσομαι (JosAs 6:2); 2 aor. ἀπεκρύβην LXX; pf. ptc. ἀποκεκρυμμένος (s. κρύπτω; Hom.+) gener. ‘put something out of sight’.

① to provide a hiding-place for someth. or someone, hide, conceal by digging Mt 25:18 v.l. A son from persecutors, GJs 22:3; 23:1.

② to keep from being known, keep secret τὶ ἀπό τινος (Is 40:27; Jer 39:17; Just., D. 55, 3) Lk 10:21; Hs 9, 11, 9. ἀποκεκρυμμένος hidden, kept secret (Pla., Phdr. 273c ἀποκεκρυμμένη τέχνη; Ps.-Demetr. c. 155 κατηγορίαι ἀποκεκρυμμέναι; Just., D. 115, 1 Ζαχαρίᾳ … ἀποκεκρυμμένως κηρύσσοντι) 1 Cor 2:7; Eph 3:9; Col 1:26.—M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

someth. someth. = something

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 114
